ops.body:
Handoff for the daemonkit extraction program as of 2026-07-19. Shipped and green: daemonkit v0.1.0 (tag a5ed2e3); fusekit v1.5.0 (the hard-cut; fusekit-holder cask published live); cc-interact 4aad58c+a0578e7; cc-orchestrate f1f15ca; cc-pool 4e1050a+b13657c+d9edb3d (migration + widget release-collapse + CI greening); cc-notes needed no flip; authkit v0.2.0 (CallerCheck pin fix); Track A live-verified end to end (one-tap Touch ID round-trip, ccn fusekit task 3a5812f done). The master plan file: ~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-08/plans/we-want-to-extracct-cozy-ocean.md.

## 1. In flight at handoff (finish first)
- **cc-pool widget release rehearsal**: rc1 exposed the missing xcconfig stub (fixed), rc2 exposed the callee's profile-resolution ordering bug (-showBuildSettings never downloads profiles; only a real build does). The fix is in flight in homebrew-tap release-app.yml (build first, then validate the BUILT products' embedded profiles); after it lands: ship the tap, retag v1, push cc-pool v0.31.0-rc3, expect release-app fully green with the cask correctly NOT published (prerelease). Then delete the rc tags/releases. The rc numbering is deliberately below the real version ceiling (v0.59.0) — harmless for prereleases.
- **Track K (captain-hook karp)**: SHIPPED 2026-07-19 (captain-hook 5d5de25f, CI green incl. the new helper-test job). Gate + review verdict: captain-hook ccn note a7f3d46; wire contract doc 66026ba; docs-needed note 7046fe1. The release-app.yml prerelease-diff-base bug found in review is fixed (homebrew-tap b6c1154, v1 advanced). The karp spec is the plan file `this-should-hav-a-curious-karp.md` (NOT wise-snuggling-karp — codename collision).
- **fusekit-holder observed cask upgrade**: v1.5.0's cask published; one observed live upgrade on a host completes Track F's last gate line (coordinate — a holder over live mounts).

## 2. Docs pass (fable prose; writing-docs + slop-cop on all of it)
- captain-hook: docs/guide/desktop-helper.qmd (+ session-reviewer.qmd cross-link), CHANGELOG entries (helper, --pr-title, review snapshot), scanning-sessions SKILL.md stamp updates to carry --pr-title. From Wave B's docs-needed list.
- cc-pool: ccn doc 7b7a53f already rewritten (newer-wins takeover + settle-before-cancel drain) — verify it against the shipped code once more at review.
- cc-interact/cc-orchestrate/cc-pool CHANGELOGs: entries for the migration behavior changes (EUID floor, 64MiB frame cap, newer-wins eviction, settle-before-cancel, KeepAlive SuccessfulExit=false).
- daemonkit docs/conventions.md + per-track docs + README refresh (the plan's cross-cutting docs line; task a9c2d88 in this store).

## 3. Wave 3 — Track S (synckit + cookiesync + reposync + host cutover)
ccn task 979cca8 in this store; the plan's Track S bullet holds the full preserved-semantics list (frozen rpc Request/Response bytes, exclusive dispatch, 16MiB cap, same-UID-before-first-byte, exit-255-for-ssh, record-before-notify anti-echo, per-peer breakers, tombstones, monotone writes) and the no-outage host-cutover runbook shape (bootout synckitd FIRST, per-app agents, TCC surfaces, squatter recovery). Apply the recon-first contract: THREE prior tracks proved the plan's migration mappings can be optimistic — verify synckit's actual shapes against daemonkit before rewiring, and expect wire.Server NOT to fit frozen-byte consumers (use Framing/PeerFromConn sub-primitives, per cc-interact's precedent and the v0.2 note af0d6e7 in the daemonkit store).

## 4. Track FD — fusekit-holder daemon on daemonkit drain (design-first)
ccn task 32df2a6 in this store. fusekit's holder keeps skew-triggered self-retire + LaunchAgent relaunch today; the drain-consumer rework is the AGENTS.md-referenced multi-tenant holder plan. Design must resolve: the frozen retire wire fields, successor-spawn vs launchd under the fork-storm constraint, mount-spec journal vs canonical seq journal. Carries the drain-consumer contract checklist (daemonkit doc 67f6e62), gated on a fable design doc before any implementation. The deferred hello/health-before-proto-gate patch sketch for mountd is on the task.

## 5. Wave 4 — security hardening
- Go XPC/Mach audit-trailer transport replacing LOCAL_PEERTOKEN on code-identity surfaces: ccn task db24393 (this store). fable implements (security-sensitive); sol reviews.
- Swift XPCTrust, TF9 (library-validation strictness), TF10 (fork/exec, PID-reuse, SCM_RIGHTS, setuid trust tests), cross-team reject fixture: daemonkit note ae67609 + P2 task 9a16f2e's one pending criterion (this store) enumerate them.
- Also from ae67609: Go reads frames before trust (revisit with XPC); Swift PeerTrust lacks Go's Hardened-Runtime/injection-entitlement parity; Swift foreign-UID floor needs a cross-uid E2E.

## 6. daemonkit v0.2 (all in the daemonkit store)
Note af0d6e7 is the roadmap: supervise's four gaps (persisted budget/ResetStrikes, confirmed-dead-without-probe, push entry, per-subject grace), Takeover's PIDExit-on-every-path + grace early-out + client identity binding, drain.Simple's cancel-order for long-lived streams, wire.Server consumer-envelope serving, KeyedMutex TryLock. Add at review time: service.Agent restart policy (SuccessfulExit:false — cc-pool adapts the plist caller-side until then), proc.Spawn ctx-check-before-launch (a dead ctx still launches today), a shared frozen-order JSON writer (three consumers now hand-serialize against Foundation's hash-ordered JSONEncoder). Plus task ea0f944 (stale appexes token in templates/release.yml.tmpl). cc-orchestrate task a315cc4 (supervisor-test teardown flake) and the cli/service.go ensureDaemon-onto-Takeover convergence (recorded on cc-pool's Track P trail) ride along.

## 7. Release-ordering loose ends
- cc-orchestrate's next release needs a cc-interact release first, then a require bump (its go.mod pins v0.11.0).
- Session task #17 (cask publish through release-app.yml specifically) is proven at cc-pool's first REAL (non-rc) release; the shared publish actions are already twice-proven (authkit, fusekit-holder).
- cookiesync's next release exercises its cask's new depends_on authkit chain.

Working discipline that carried the program: fetch-and-check origin before dispatching any track (memory: verify-track-state-before-dispatch); recon-first with stop-and-options on structural mismatch; sol refuter over every implementation before ship; disk is the record; ships via ccx vcs ship with hunk-scoping for shared working copies.
